title: Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez
sub: The highest race on the calendar at 2,240m, where thin air saps downforce and cooling and sends cars to their highest top speeds. Ends with the unique stadium section through the old Foro Sol, roaring with 40,000 fans.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: The huge main straight, extended by the thin air, into the Turn 1-2-3 braking complex, then the fast Turn 4 and the Esses.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: A mid-speed sequence through the infield: the Turn 7-8 Esses and the run to the slow Turn 11, the tightest part of the lap.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: The unique stadium section through the old Foro Sol baseball park, hemmed by grandstands on three sides, then the old Peraltada banking (now Turn 16-17) onto the straight.

tyreAlloc: Middle of the range · C2 / C3 / C4
stratNote: The thin air is the whole story: less downforce means low mechanical grip, long braking, and cooling headaches, but also the year’s highest top speeds down the enormous straight. Overheating brakes and power units shape the race as much as tyre wear.
spots: End of the enormous main straight + DRS. Cars arrive three-wide; the biggest braking and passing zone.
A secondary dive after a short DRS straight.
A late lunge on the run into the infield.
otNote: The gigantic straight (lengthened by the thin air) plus three DRS zones make Turn 1 a multi-car braking zone every lap. Overtaking is genuinely good; the low grip just makes it messy.
corners: End of the huge straight + DRS. Long braking in thin air; three-wide chaos and the prime overtaking spot.
Quick left-right out of the Turn 1 complex.
Quick right after a short DRS straight; a secondary move.
Heavy braking into the infield; a late-lunge chance.
The tightest corner on the lap; traction-critical.
Slow corners through the old baseball stadium, grandstands roaring on three sides. The atmosphere highlight of the year.
The remains of the famous Peraltada banking, now the final corners firing cars onto the long main straight.
geo: At 2,240m the highest circuit on the calendar. The thin air cuts aerodynamic downforce by roughly a fifth and starves brakes and power units of cooling, a unique engineering puzzle.
Despite max-downforce wings, the thin air means low drag, so cars hit the year’s highest speeds (~350+ km/h) down the vast main straight.
The lap threads through a former baseball stadium, hemmed by grandstands on three sides. The noise and colour make it one of F1’s great atmospheric moments.
Named for the Rodríguez brothers, and now the fervent home of Sergio Pérez’s fans. A raucous, sold-out fiesta every year.
